tldr: Nichole, all five of your live ads are past their refresh window — three are sitting at 71 days running, and the same 'NYC vintage shopping + custom Italian charm bracelet = elite combo' line is pasted verbatim across two of your videos. Bigger picture: you're running 2 distinct concepts across 5 ads, which sits below Meta's Andromeda 5-concept floor and is actively suppressing reach.

benchmark.context: Other vintage fashion brands at your scale typically refresh hero creative around every 21 days; the top performers are at 14. Your active set has been untouched for 64-71 days, and you're sitting at 2 distinct concepts — Meta's Andromeda retrieval engine treats anything below 5 concepts as a single cluster, which compresses delivery regardless of how many ads you stack on top.

ads.body: NYC vintage shopping + custom Italian charm bracelet = elite combo.
drivers: Body copy is identical to another live video — same one-line hook, no variation. Andromeda clusters these as a single ad.
No headline on this ad — the body is doing all the work, and a single-line hook with no headline gives Meta nothing to A/B at the asset level.
64 days running, more than double your 28-day refresh window. The cluster has been served past the point of diminishing returns.

ads.body: NYC vintage shopping + custom Italian charm bracelet = elite combo.
drivers: Verbatim duplicate of another live video — Andromeda's retrieval clustering treats these as the same creative, so you're paying for two impressions of one concept.
No headline field filled — same gap as its twin, which means neither ad benefits from headline-level diversity.
71 days in market with no refresh. Vintage/lifestyle creative typically loses pattern-interrupt power around day 21-28.

ads.headline: instagram.com
ads.body: Warning: you're about to become a regular 👀 Say hello to your new favorite vintage store. Fresh vintage and secondhand finds for women + men and home decor that sparks joy. Come visit and see for yourself  📍 Upper East Side - 311 E 81st  📍Upper West Side -  2206 Broadway
drivers: Headline auto-fills as 'instagram.com' — that's the URL pulling through, not a written headline. You're losing your strongest above-the-fold real estate.
Same store-visit framing and same two-address footer as another live static — copy reads as a duplicate concept with light rewording.
71 days running on a static format. Static creative without rotation is the fastest cluster to fatigue under Andromeda.

ads.headline: instagram.com
ads.body: We don't gatekeep! Colorful racks. Fun finds. Women's, men's, accessories, Italian charms, and home decor waiting for you. Come visit and see what everyone's talking about  📍 Upper East Side - 311 E 81st  📍Upper West Side -  2206 Broadway
drivers: Headline is the URL ('instagram.com') instead of a written line — Meta pulls the domain when you leave the field blank, and it costs you the headline slot entirely.
Identical address block and store-visit CTA as the other static. Two ads, same concept, light copy variation = one cluster in Andromeda's eyes.
71 days in flight. The 'Come visit' angle has been the only foot-traffic message running for 10+ weeks straight.

ads.body: Finding and sharing the best vintage in NYC. One-of-a-kind pieces you won't find anywhere else. Stop by and discover your next favorite fit.
drivers: No headline written — the video has to carry both the hook and the headline slot, which weakens stop-rate on feed scroll.
Body recycles the same 'NYC vintage / one-of-a-kind / come visit' angle that runs across the rest of the active set. No second concept lane.
71 days running. Past every refresh benchmark — your own 28-day window, the category median, and the top-quartile bar.

hero_concept.hook: The charm everyone keeps asking about.
hero_concept.headline: Build it. Wear it. Keep it.
hero_concept.primary_text:
Every week, one Italian charm walks out of the shop more than the others. This week it's the Music Note — soldered onto wrists from the Upper East Side to the Upper West.

The charm bar is open seven days a week at both stores. Pick your bracelet, pick your charms, walk out wearing it. No assembly line, no waiting on shipping — it's built in front of you.

📍 311 E 81st  📍 2206 Broadway. Stop in this weekend.
hero_concept.visual_direction: Macro overhead shot of the Music Note charm on a soft cream linen surface beside an in-progress charm bracelet, shallow depth of field, golden-hour window light.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Your active set has zero product-forward creative — every ad is either a wide store hype-video or an address-and-emoji static, with the charm bracelet only mentioned as a one-liner. A close-up product shot of the actual hero SKU opens a second concept lane and gets you above the Andromeda 5-concept floor.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a macro overhead flat-lay on a cream linen surface with one in-progress charm bracelet softly out of focus in the background, with warm golden-hour window light raking from the upper left and casting soft natural shadows. Editorial vintage-shop aesthetic — warm, tactile, hand-crafted, mirroring the curated 'one-of-a-kind' feel of the brand's own video creative. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL — preserve the exact gold-tone Italian charm metal finish, the exact engraved music-note motif, and the exact polished surface treatment shown in the reference; strictly NO silver, white-gold, rose-gold, brass-shift, or warm-to-cool tone bias, and NO recoloring of the charm body. STRUCTURE — preserve the exact construction: a single Italian charm tile featuring the engraved music-note (eighth-note / quaver) motif on the front face, with the same charm-tile shape, the same edge profile, and the same connector loops/links on the sides exactly as shown in the reference. Do NOT add or remove charm links, do NOT change the music-note engraving into a different symbol, do NOT alter the tile shape, do NOT add gemstones or enamel that aren't present, do NOT modify the connector hardware. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself — text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: Two of your ads hit cluster fatigue inside the next 5 days.
next_step.headline: Walk through the The Niche Shop refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Nichole, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep — work with us or don't.

method_note: We went through every live ad in your Meta Ad Library and mapped concept count, copy duplication, format mix, and days-running against current Andromeda retrieval rules. No private account data was used.
